Arriola_gis

Arriola, Phoebe M.

2026-06-22

library(maps)
library(osmdata)
library(sf)
library(terra)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggnewscale)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Read the raw data
getwd()
## [1] "/Users/phoebearriola/Desktop/Uni"
setwd("/Users/phoebearriola/Desktop/uni")
list.files()
##  [1] "[SCG-PRE-ENLISTMENT]T3_AY25-26.pdf" "~$CELLMOL.docx"                    
##  [3] "~$SBIOGEO.docx"                     "0073014-260519110011954.csv"       
##  [5] "Arriola_gis.Rmd"                    "Arriola_hclust.html"               
##  [7] "Arriola_hclust.Rmd"                 "Arriola_PCA.html"                  
##  [9] "Arriola_PCA.Rmd"                    "Enrollment files_CSAG"             
## [11] "Icon\r"                             "Meneses_et.al_2026_Platymantis.csv"
## [13] "morphology_data.csv"                "Notes_Term 3 AY 25-26"             
## [15] "ph_ strm_geo.tif"                   "PHL_adm"                           
## [17] "PHL_adm.zip"                        "PHL_adm1.dbf"                      
## [19] "PHL_adm1.prj"                       "PHL_adm1.shp"                      
## [21] "PHL_adm1.shx"
df <- read.delim("0073014-260519110011954.csv") 

# 2. Filter for complete coordinate records
good <- complete.cases(df[, c("decimalLongitude", "decimalLatitude")])
cleaned <- df[good, ]

# 3. Read the provincial level shapefile
my_shape <- st_read("PHL_adm1.shp")
## Reading layer `PHL_adm1' from data source 
##   `/Users/phoebearriola/Desktop/Uni/PHL_adm1.shp' using driver `ESRI Shapefile'
## Simple feature collection with 81 features and 9 fields
## Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON
## Dimension:     XY
## Bounding box:  xmin: 116.9283 ymin: 4.58694 xmax: 126.6053 ymax: 21.07014
## Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84
# 4. Filter for the species' range in Northern Luzon
luzon_provinces <- my_shape %>%
  filter(NAME_1 %in% c("Cagayan", "Isabela", "Ilocos Norte", "Abra", 
                       "Kalinga", "Apayao", "Mountain Province", 
                       "Nueva Vizcaya", "Quirino"))

# 5. Load the Digital Elevation Model raster
phil_dem <- rast("ph_ strm_geo.tif") 

# 6. Check and match the Coordinate Reference Systems (CRS)
if (st_crs(luzon_provinces) != st_crs(phil_dem)) {
  luzon_provinces <- st_transform(luzon_provinces, st_crs(phil_dem))
}

# 7. Clip and mask the elevation to selected provinces
dem <- crop(phil_dem, luzon_provinces) |> mask(luzon_provinces)

# 8. Convert the raster into a dataframe so ggplot can plot 
names(dem) <- "elevation"
r_df <- as.data.frame(dem, xy = TRUE)

# 9. Building the map with ggplolt2
ggplot() +
  # a. Background Layer: Elevation Raster
  geom_tile(data = r_df, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = elevation)) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("#2b83ba", "#abdda4", "#ffffbf", "#fdae61", "#d7191c"),
    name = "Elevation (m)") +
    
  # b. Reset the fill scale to add species points cleanly
    new_scale_fill() +
    
  # c. Top Layer: Species occurrence points from GBIF
geom_point(data = cleaned,
             aes(x = decimalLongitude, y = decimalLatitude, fill = "Northern Luzon Giant Cloud Rat"),
             shape = 21, 
             color = "black", 
             size = 3, 
             stroke = 1.2) +
    # d. Map Customization and Labels
scale_fill_manual(values = c("Northern Luzon Giant Cloud Rat" = "#1b9e77"), name = "Species") +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(119.5, 122.5), 
           ylim = c(15.5, 18.5), 
           expand = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Distribution Map of Phloeomys pallidus",
       subtitle = "Northern Luzon, Philippines",
       x = "Longitude (°E)",
       y = "Latitude (°N)") +
  theme_bw()
